title: Dry Roasted Thai Shrimp Tom Yum
description: A high-protein Thai dish featuring roasted shrimp in a tom yum flavored sauce with garam masala, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the tom yum paste, garam masala, fish sauce, and lime juice.
Add the shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat with the tom yum mixture.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the shrimp in a single layer.
Drizzle the vegetable oil over the shrimp and sprinkle with bruised lemon grass and kaffir lime leaves.
Roast the shrimp in the preheated oven for 8 minutes.
Remove the shrimp from the oven and broil for an additional 2 minutes, or until crispy.
Serve the shrimp hot, garnished with additional kaffir lime leaves if desired.
